title: Cestrum toledii
taxonomy: Cestrum toledii is classified within the family Solanaceae, which is commonly known as the nightshade family. This family comprises over 2,800 species of flowering plants, including many economically important species such as tomatoes and potatoes. The genus Cestrum is a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plants within this genus are known for their showy white or yellow flowers, which are often fragrant and bloom in the evening. Cestrum toledii, in particular, is a species of flowering plant that is native to the Andes mountains in Colombia.
uses: Cestrum toledii is reportedly used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts are said to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In some regions, Cestrum toledii is used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. Additionally, it is believed to have wound-healing properties and is used to treat various infections. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Cestrum toledii for medicinal use.

side_effects: Cestrum toledii, also known as the Purple Queen, is a plant native to South America and has been reported to cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals due to its sap and leaves. When handled improperly, the plant's sap can cause skin burns, blisters, and rashes. Ingesting any part of the plant can lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The plant's toxicity can also cause respiratory problems and eye irritation in some cases. It is essential to handle the plant with caution and wear protective gear when working with it to avoid any potential side effects.
intro: Cestrum toledii, also known as the Toledano night-blooming jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the family Solanaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it effective in reducing pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as treating respiratory issues and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Cestrum toledii include alkaloids, such as toledin, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
